truth.conflictRule: Resolve each fact at its owning truth home; if sources disagree, change the owning source and regenerate GroundAtlas rather than editing generated maps.

homes.owns: What the project is, lifecycle, owner boundary, public surfaces, adoption state, and delivery proof.
Architecture, product, data, security, operations, or commercial decisions that must survive beyond one edit.
Requirements, adoption rules, operating models, design intent, and acceptance criteria.
Schemas, type contracts, migrations, package exports, command surfaces, and dependency boundaries.
What the product actually does at runtime.
Expected behavior, regressions, evals, validation commands, and release gates.
Repeatable procedures, vulnerability reporting, incident handling, and customer support expectations.

orientation.title: Agent adapter
orientation.reason: Start with local agent rules so automation follows the repository boundary.
orientation.ssotRole: Runtime adapter; prefer AGENTS.md as the tool-neutral entry point and treat tool-specific files as optional adapters.

orientation.title: Project identity and machine manifest
orientation.reason: Find the project goal, lifecycle, owner boundary, public surfaces, and adoption state.
orientation.ssotRole: Canonical truth for project identity and boundaries.

orientation.title: Public start-here documentation
orientation.reason: Read the product promise, install path, and user-facing contract.
orientation.ssotRole: Canonical public entry point; lower authority than schemas, tests, ADRs, and source.

orientation.title: Specs and design intent
orientation.reason: Understand intended behavior, adoption rules, and design constraints before editing.
orientation.ssotRole: Canonical for intended product/operating contracts until implemented facts disagree.

orientation.title: Architecture and decision records
orientation.reason: Find durable choices and trade-offs before inventing a new pattern.
orientation.ssotRole: Canonical for durable decisions; newer ADRs supersede older conflicting prose.

orientation.title: Machine contracts and build surfaces
orientation.reason: Identify commands, exports, schemas, and machine-readable contracts.
orientation.ssotRole: Canonical for package, schema, API, and type contracts.

orientation.title: Implementation and behavior proof
orientation.reason: Verify how the product actually behaves and what regressions are protected.
orientation.ssotRole: Canonical for implemented behavior and expected behavior.

orientation.title: CI, release, security, and operations
orientation.reason: Prove the delivery path, release gates, operational procedures, and support boundary.
orientation.ssotRole: Canonical for validation automation, release operations, and support procedures.

sources.reason: Records durable architecture or product decisions.
Bootstraps agents into repo-local context; AGENTS.md is preferred and tool-specific adapters are optional.
Defines package metadata, binaries, scripts, and dependency surface.
Defines repository identity, lifecycle, boundary, or project-local truth.
Defines repository identity, lifecycle, boundary, or project-local truth.
Proves expected behavior or regression coverage.

validationCommands.reason: package.json defines the check validation script.
package.json defines the test validation script.
package.json defines the build validation script.

risks.message: README.md is missing; public human entry point is not explicit.
No docs/specs/ or DESIGN.md was discovered; product intent may not be reviewable.
No GitHub workflow was discovered; validation may rely on local discipline.
SECURITY.md is missing; vulnerability reporting and security expectations are unclear.
